Guides

Skills & Drills

Improvement is easier when practice is sequenced. Instead of scattered tips, these articles are placed along a DUPR-level pathway — what to work on at 2.0–3.0, what unlocks 3.5, and the habits that separate 4.0+ players. Start where you are and follow the path.

Skills & Drills

What a DUPR 2.5 player looks like — and how to reach 3.0

A plain description of a 2.5 player, the consistency gaps that define the level, and the steps that lead to 3.0.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Skills & Drills

From 3.5 to 4.0: the habits you have to fix

The specific habits that cap a 3.5 player, and how fixing patience, resets, targeting, and shot selection unlocks 4.0.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Skills & Drills

From 3.0 to 3.5: a focused improvement plan

The specific shots and habits that separate 3.5 from 3.0, plus a simple practice structure to build them.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Skills & Drills

Serve and return basics: common mistakes and fixes

Simple ways to make your serve and return consistent and deep.

Updated Jun 21, 2026 · Level 2.0–3.0
Skills & Drills

The third-shot drop, explained: why it matters and how to drill it

What the third-shot drop is, when to use it versus a drive, and how to practise it.

Updated Jun 21, 2026 · Level 3.0–3.5
Skills & Drills

Dinking fundamentals: staying steady at the kitchen line

What a good dink looks like, the technique behind it, and how to practise patience at the net.

Updated Jun 21, 2026 · Level 3.0
Skills & Drills

Your first 30 days: a practice routine for 2.0–3.0 players

A four-week practice framework for new players, focused on the few skills that raise your level fastest.

Updated Jun 21, 2026 · Level 2.0–3.0
